parameters.description: Length of the pUC19 cloning vector used as the task substrate in Clone-01.

citations.authors: Yanisch-Perron C, Vieira J, Messing J
citations.title: Improved M13 phage cloning vectors and host strains: nucleotide sequences of the M13mp18 and pUC19 vectors
citations.tier_justification: Peer-reviewed paper reporting the pUC19 sequence and its 2686 bp length.

parameters.description: Length of the benign PCR-amplified insert provided as a Clone-01 substrate, with EcoRI and BamHI flanking sites.

citations.title: NEB Double-Digest Finder (EcoRI and BamHI)
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor reference confirming EcoRI + BamHI as a standard directional cloning pair used for routine PCR-insert cloning; the 950 bp insert length is a Clone-01 scenario configuration.

parameters.description: Buffers compatible with simultaneous EcoRI and BamHI digestion at high efficiency.

citations.title: NEB Double-Digest Finder
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor chart listing CutSmart / rCutSmart / NEB 3.1 as the recommended buffers for EcoRI + BamHI double digests at 100% activity.

parameters.description: Incubation temperature for EcoRI and BamHI digestion.

citations.title: NEB EcoRI-HF product page
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative vendor product page listing 37 C as the optimal incubation temperature.

parameters.description: Minimum incubation time for complete EcoRI + BamHI double digestion of plasmid substrates.

citations.title: NEB Cloning and Synthetic Biology: Double Digest Protocol
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB protocol recommending at least one hour of incubation for complete plasmid digestion.

parameters.description: Temperature used to heat-inactivate EcoRI and BamHI after digestion.

citations.title: NEB Heat Inactivation Reference
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB reference listing 65 C as the heat-inactivation temperature for both EcoRI and BamHI.

parameters.description: Ligase required for ATP-dependent cohesive-end ligation of EcoRI and BamHI-digested fragments.

citations.title: Enzymatic breakage and joining of deoxyribonucleic acid. I. Repair of single-strand breaks in DNA by an enzyme system from Escherichia coli infected with T4 bacteriophage
citations.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
citations.tier_justification: Foundational peer-reviewed description of T4 DNA ligase activity.

parameters.description: Canonical vector:insert molar ratio for sticky-end ligation with T4 DNA ligase.

citations.title: NEB T4 DNA Ligase Protocol (M0202)
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB protocol recommending a 1:3 vector:insert molar ratio (insert in threefold excess) for sticky-end ligations.

parameters.description: Temperatures at which T4 DNA ligase performs cohesive-end ligation efficiently.

citations.title: NEB T4 DNA Ligase Protocol (M0202)
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB protocol noting that T4 DNA ligase ligations are typically run overnight at 16 C or at room temperature (22-25 C).

parameters.description: Minimum acceptable ligation incubation time before transformation.

citations.title: NEB Quick Ligation Protocol (M2200)
citations.tier_justification: Authoritative NEB Quick Ligation protocol supporting 10 minutes at 25 C as an acceptable minimum duration.

parameters.description: Baseline recombinant fraction among white colonies when Clone-01 is performed with correct chemistry, matching the Screen-01 prior to keep downstream statistics consistent.

citations.title: NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook of Statistical Methods: Binomial Distribution
citations.tier_justification: Official NIST reference for the binomial model used consistently across Screen-01 and Clone-01 to keep downstream confidence calculations comparable.
